data.title: Dow Jones Slips as PCE Inflation Surprises to the Upside
data.description:

data.title: Here's Why Mangoceuticals Stock (MGRX) Rocketed Today
data.description:
Mangoceuticals (MGRX) stock underwent a massive rally on Friday after the men's wellness company announced successful results from its new Testosterone Replacement Therapy (TRT) treatment program. This program offers all-inclusive injectable TRT, doctor visits, medical consultations, lab work and analysis for $99 per month.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mangoceuticals noted that its month-over-month sales for its injectable TRT treatment program have increased 336% since it launched in mid-December. Additionally, the company said that it has reduced customer acquisition costs by 54%. Mangoceuticals said it expects the injectable TRT treatment program to be its primary growth driver moving forward.

Jacob Cohen, Founder and CEO of Mangoceuticals, said, "By combining effective TRT solutions with streamlined telehealth delivery and targeted cost efficiencies, we are seeking to become better positioned to support more men seeking reliable, discreet care. At the same time, we are committed to building a more comprehensive platform that delivers a wider range of wellness solutions, which we expect to strengthen our operational and financial foundation and position us for sustained growth."

Mangoceuticals Stock Movement Today

Mangoceuticals stock was up 68.86% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 1.08% drop yesterday. The shares have fallen 51.88% year-to-date and 91.78% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of MGRX stock. This saw more than 122 million shares change hands as of this writing. For comparison, the stock's three-month daily average trading volume is about 208,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Barclays Bank (BCS) Names Four Pharma Stocks to Buy Now
data.description:
Barclays Bank (BCS) has initiated coverage of U.S. large-cap pharmaceutical companies with four recommendations that the bank says are immediate buys.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analyst Emily Field writes in a note to clients that the U.S. pharma sector is likely to regain attention as investors and traders rotate out of richly-valued technology stocks and seek safety amid an uncertain economic and geopolitical environment.

The four pharma stocks that Field recommends investors buy now are Eli Lilly (LLY), Merck (MRK), Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY), and AbbVie (ABBV). The analyst writes that these pharma stocks offer "a relatively safe haven in times of uncertainty" and could be an area investors "will come back home to in 2026."

Catalysts for Pharma Stocks

Field also points to several catalysts that could boost the pharma sector in coming months. These include most-favored-nation agreements with the U.S., aging demographics, rising chronic disease prevalence, and continued growth in healthcare spending, notably in America.

The Barclays analyst also highlights that pharma's defensive qualities are being rediscovered, noting that the pharmaceutical stocks have historically outperformed the broader market during times of turmoil and major drawdowns.

Among the four pharma stocks recommended, Field named Eli Lilly as Barclays' top pick. She views obesity treatments as a "durable structural shift" and says that Eli Lilly is likely to remain the market leader with the launch of its weight-loss pill this summer.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,263.78 implies 23.51% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Super Micro Computer Stock (SMCI) Jumped 8%
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI), a maker of servers used in AI data centers and cloud networks, jumped 8% to close at $32.16 on Thursday. The move looked sudden, especially since there was no company announcement. The real trigger came from the options market. However, the stock is trading lower in early hours on Friday.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Call Buying Drives the Rally

A large spike in call options — bets that the stock will go up — pushed SMCI shares to about $32 and back above the 50-day moving average, a level many traders follow closely.

By 10:45 a.m. ET, about 114,000 call contracts had traded. That is already a large amount for early trading and puts the stock on track to beat its normal daily activity.

The tone was clearly bullish:

  • The put-to-call ratio fell below 0.16, much lower than the recent average near 0.38
  • Most trades focused on options expiring this Friday and next Friday
  • The busiest strike prices were $32 to $33.50, right around the current stock price

In short, many traders are betting the stock will rise in the very near term.

The next step is whether the stock can hold these gains. Investors will watch if shares stay above the 50-day moving average and whether the options activity continues. If not, the move could cool just as fast as it started.

Is SMCI Stock a Buy? 

Last month, Super Micro delivered a strong fiscal second quarter, beating Wall Street estimates across the board. Adjusted earnings came in at $0.69 per share, well above forecasts of $0.49, while revenue surged to $12.7 billion, topping expectations of $10.42 billion. The results highlight strong demand for AI servers.

Following the earnings release, Rosenblatt Securities analyst Kevin Cassidy reiterated a Buy rating on Super Micro with a $50 price target. He said results were driven by a sharp ramp in AI system production and that he expects demand to remain strong. Cassidy also sees room for margin gains as the company expands its "One-Stop Shop" data center offering, supported by low operating costs.

On TipRanks, SMCI has a Hold consensus rating based on two Buys, six Holds, and two Sell ratings. The average Super Micro Computer price target of $38.89 implies 20.93% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Starbucks Stock (SBUX) Loses Froth as it Ramps Up Store Expansion in Loss-Making India
data.description:
Shares in coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) lost some froth today as it looked to double-down its store openings in India despite increasing losses in the world's most populous nation.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Starbucks Local Tastes

According to a report in Bloomberg, Starbucks' India arm – a joint-venture with Tata called Tata Starbucks – is looking to add more stores across India despite the unit struggling to make a profit.

Not only is it adding more stores, said the report, it is also looking to re-jig its menu to appeal more to Indian customers. That means local-style offerings such as protein-foam coffee paired with Mumbai-style buns, chai-offerings and more value-focused drinks.

Tata Starbucks ended 2025 with 504 stores across 81 cities in India making it the biggest operator in the country. It added twelve stores during the third quarter ended 31 December, and achieved 7% year-on-year revenue growth. It has a goal of hitting 1,000 stores by 2028. However, losses nearly doubled to $16.5 million in the year to March.

"India remains a focus area as much if not more," said Tata Starbucks Chief Executive Officer Sushant Dash. He added that it will now prioritize expansion over profitability as investment accelerates.

This shows the potential Starbucks executives see in India as a strategic growth market – and why not? It has nearly 1.5 billion people and a burgeoning coffee market.

According to reports, the India Coffee Market was valued at $478 million in 2022 and is expected to reach $1,227.47 million by 2032.

Domestic Drinking Squeeze

Domestic coffee consumption in India has increased substantially in the last five years owing to the growing middle class and increasing awareness of coffee culture. The branded coffee and specialty café market in India is projected to grow at a double‑digit rate into the next decade.

There are challenges however such as rising competition from local chains and lower-cost formats. India's economic slowdown is another factor with ongoing inflation, flat wages and consumers – especially the urban middle class – feeling the squeeze.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Edge Lower Ahead of Key Inflation Data, Tariff Ruling
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended lower on Friday morning ahead of the key Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index and a possible ruling by the Supreme Court on President Donald Trump's tariffs. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.08%, 0.05%, and 0.07%, respectively, at 8:25 a.m. EST on February 20.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major averages closed lower amid rising geopolitical tensions. The DJIA and the S&P 500 declined 0.54% and 0.28%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.31%. Stocks snapped a three-day winning streak for both the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones, with oil prices reaching a six-month high amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions.

Turning to economic data, traders await key releases, including the fourth-quarter GDP, projected at 2.5% growth following Q3's stronger-than-expected 4.4% rise. Also, economists forecast a 2.8% annual gain in headline PCE and 3% in core PCE.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Is AIOS Tech Stock Up Today?
data.description:
AIOS Tech (AIOS) stock underwent a massive rally on Friday despite a lack of news from the artificial intelligence (AI) and technology-driven professional services company. There are no press releases or filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) that explain why the stock rallied today.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Instead, it looks like retail traders are behind today's activity. AIOS stock has experienced heavy trading in the pre-market, with more than 22 million shares traded. To put that in perspective, the company's three-month daily average trading volume is about 24,000 units.

Investors will also note that the interest in AIOS Tech comes after a recent transformation. The company has changed its focus to AI services from financing solutions. This also resulted in a name and ticker change last week. Prior to this, the company was named Nisun International Enterprise Development Group and traded under the NISN ticker.

AIOS Tech Stock Movement Today

AIOS Tech stock was up 49.02% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 3.05% rally yesterday. However, the stock has fallen 57.94% year-to-date and 94.16% over the past 12 months.

The volatility that AIOS Tech stock has experienced is a red flag, as is its shift to AI. It suggests the company is chasing a trend amid failure, rather than making a well-thought-out and strategic shift.
data.author: William White
